messages.content:
Your security rules seem to be correctly allowing all users to read posts from the "FutureMK" collection, but only the admin can write to it. However, it's possible that the collection is currently empty and that's why you're not seeing any posts.

Make sure that you have at least one document in the "FutureMK" collection with either an authenticated user ID as the document ID or a field that is accessible to all users, such as a "public" or "visible" field set to true.

You can add a document to the collection manually from the Firebase console or programmatically with one of the SDKs. Once there is at least one document in the collection, you should be able to read it from any user account.
[2023-06-07T04:55:54.001Z]  @firebase/firestore: Firestore (9.22.1): Uncaught Error in snapshot listener: FirebaseError: [code=permission-denied]: Missing or insufficient permissions.

Your current Firebase security rules look correct for allowing all users to read data from the `FutureMK` collection and only authenticated users with admin privileges to write data to it. 

Authenticated users should be able to write data to the `FutureMK` collection if they have an `admin` field set to `true` in their `users` document. Make sure that you are setting this field properly when creating new users and updating existing users.

Also, keep in mind that these security rules only apply to users who are properly authenticated with Firebase. If you are receiving `permission-denied` errors when trying to access Firestore data from your app, make sure that you are properly authenticating your users with Firebase and that they are authorized to access the requested data.

If you continue to have issues with your security rules, you can use the Firebase console to test your rules and identify any errors or issues with your setup. This can help you troubleshoot any issues and ensure that your security rules are set up correctly.
my user now is a guest and he cant see info of the posts
i dont understand where u r writing. when i write this code in firebase realtime db it says i have errs
do i need to update cloudstore too?

It depends on your application. If you're using Cloud Firestore as well as Realtime Database, you should update the security rules for both databases to ensure that your users are properly authorized to read and write data.

If you're only using one of the databases, you only need to update the security rules for that particular database. 

Make sure to review your security rules carefully and test them thoroughly to ensure that they meet your application's security requirements and prevent unauthorized access to your database.
